R1.notes: Q1 is now tight. §2 opens with an explicit definition of confluence (G7 closed): 'R is confluent iff for every term t and every pair u, v with t ->> u and t ->> v, there exists a w with u ->> w and v ->> w', and the refutation strategy ('distinct normal forms have no common reduct') is argued. The normal-form argument (G6 closed) now correctly reasons via head-symbol equality: 'Every rule LHS in R has a non-nullary head (g, m, f, or u); matching requires head-symbol equality; c's only position is eps with head c; no rule LHS has head c; therefore c is a normal form.' The case d is handled symmetrically. Distinctness c != d is stated explicitly, and the 'no common reduct' inference is spelled out line by line. Oracle cross-check cites the Q1 banner in sim/output-final.txt. Minor potential nit: the phrase 'at any position of c' could be elaborated to note c has no proper subterms, but the reasoning is unambiguous from context.

R2.notes: Q2 is substantially tightened across all previously flagged dimensions. §3.1 defines the strategy S; §3.2 proves S's totality on non-normal-form closed terms via explicit case analysis (rho_i != rho6 vs rho_i = rho6, with the latter pointing to the shared rho5/rho6 LHS f(?x)) — G5 closed. §3.3 defines W by structural recursion on every symbol of Sigma, proves totality by case exhaustion, and types W as ClosedTerms(Sigma) -> Z_{>0} — G8 closed. §3.4 tabulates per-rule diffs and verifies rho2 in full detail; the simulator cross-check (500 random instances per non-rho6 rule, 200 for rho6) substantiates the diff signs. §3.5 states and proves a monotonicity lemma (coefficients of each argument are positive integers: 1 for s/f/u, 2/1 for g, 1/1 for m) and runs a full structural induction on one-hole contexts — G3 closed. §3.6 explicitly invokes well-foundedness of (Z_{>0}, <) with justification ('any nonempty set of positive integers has a minimum') — G4 closed. §3.7 ties S's halting condition to normal forms. §3.8 assembles the verdict. Oracle cross-check cites twelve sample runs.

R3.notes: The core G1 defect is fixed. §4 introduces body_n = f^n(c) with a clean recurrence body_0 = c, body_{k+1} = f(body_k), hence t_n = f(body_n) = f^{n+1}(c). Applying rho6 at the root of t_n with sigma = {?x := body_n} yields f(f(body_n)); the proof then invokes body_{n+1} = f(body_n) to conclude f(f(body_n)) = f(body_{n+1}) = t_{n+1}. This matches the simulator's Q3 banner (t_0..t_8 with size 2..10, all generated by rho6 at root). The general inductive step is stated cleanly and the first five concrete steps spell out the substitution at each n. Size bound size(t_n) = n+2 is correct. Oracle cross-check (G9) cites the Q3 banner explicitly. One minor blemish: line 312 displays 'f(f(body_n)) * sigma-result' as a notational remnant — since body_n is closed, applying sigma to the RHS template f(f(?x)) with ?x := body_n literally yields f(f(body_n)), so the '* sigma-result' has no content; the text immediately clarifies 'The substituted RHS is f(f(body_n)).' Minor R4-style wart but not a mathematical defect.

R4.notes: G10 closed: §5 ('Rigor parity disclosure') explicitly walks through the five shared structural elements (definitions up front, witness/strategy, explicit rule citations including position and substitution, internal verifications, oracle) across all three Qs, and addresses the structural asymmetry (Q2 is longer because it proves a universal statement; Q1 and Q3 are existential). Each Q now shares a consistent verdict -> witness/strategy -> verification -> oracle cross-check skeleton. Minor unevenness: Q2 is considerably more detailed than Q1/Q3 (as structurally necessary), and the §4 'sigma-result' line (noted in R3) is a small stylistic blemish relative to the §2 and §3 text.

R5.notes: Zero banned identifiers. Case-insensitive word-boundary scan against the full banned list (SKI, SK calculus, BCKW, BCIW, Iota, Jot, Unlambda, BLC, X-combinator, Xi-combinator, Zot, Turing's universal combinator, Curry, Schoenfinkel, Rosser, Church, Church-Rosser, Tait-Martin-Loef, Takahashi, Newman's lemma, Knuth-Bendix, Hindley-Rosen, Dershowitz, Manna-Ness, RPO, LPO, MPO, dependency pair(s), Kamin, Levy, Huet, Klop, Barendregt, Girard, Tait's computability, Plotkin, de Bruijn, recursive/lexicographic/multiset path order) returns no hits. No 'combinator'/'computability'/'universal combinator' phrases either.

R6.notes: Argument remains first-principles throughout. No named theorems are invoked; concepts like 'well-foundedness of (Z_{>0}, <)' are proved from primitives ('any nonempty set of positive integers has a minimum'). The monotonicity lemma in §3.5 is stated and justified internally rather than appealed to as an external result. Measure is built from scratch via a single recursive scheme without external provenance.

R7.notes: G2 closed. The header oracle banner at the top of ARGUMENT.md announces the simulator's role and the four categories of checks it performs. Each of §2, §3 (twice: once for rule-sampling, once for strategy-termination samples), and §4 carries an explicit '> Oracle cross-check' block citing the banner string under which the corresponding assertion lives in sim/output-final.txt. The simulator was re-run (all assertions pass). Only minor imperfection: the cross-check text cites banners rather than line numbers, but since each banner is unique this is unambiguous.

G1.notes: §4 introduces body_n = f^n(c) and proves t_n = f(body_n) -> f(f(body_n)) = f(body_{n+1}) = t_{n+1}. The previous bogus identification f(f(t_n)) = t_{n+1} is gone; the correct chain f(f(body_n)) = f(body_{n+1}) = t_{n+1} is what the text asserts. Verified against simulator: t_n = f^{n+1}(c) with rho6 at root producing t_{n+1} = f^{n+2}(c).

G2.notes: Header (lines 8-15) announces the simulator's role and what it certifies. Inline oracle cross-check blocks appear after Q1 (§2), inside Q2 (§3.4 for random-sample measure check, §3.8 for strategy-termination samples), and after Q3 (§4). §5 reiterates oracle use in its parity summary.

G3.notes: §3.5 states a 'strict monotonicity of W under each symbol' lemma with arity-by-arity coefficient enumeration, then gives a structural induction on one-hole contexts with base case (C = Hole) and inductive step (C = phi(t_1,...,C'[-],...,t_k)) invoking the monotonicity lemma. A 4-point proof sketch.

G4.notes: §3.6 states: '([·]: ClosedTerms(Sigma) -> Z_{>0}) maps into the positive integers under the usual order <. This order is well-founded: any nonempty set of positive integers has a minimum, so no infinite strictly descending sequence of positive integers exists.' Exactly the one-sentence argument requested.

G5.notes: §3.2 'S is total on non-normal-form closed terms' provides the two-case argument: if the redex's rule is not rho6, S picks it; if it is rho6, since rho5 shares the LHS f(?x), the very same match witnesses a rho5-redex at the same position, which S picks. Conclusion: 'Either way, a non-rho6 redex exists.'

G6.notes: §2 'Both endpoints are normal forms' now reasons via head-symbol equality: 'Every rule LHS in R has a non-nullary head: rho1 and rho2 have head g; rho3 and rho4 have head m; rho5 and rho6 have head f; rho7 has head u. Matching requires head-symbol equality.' Then: c's only position is eps with head c; no rule LHS has head c; c is a normal form. d is handled symmetrically.

G7.notes: §2 opens with 'What we refute', giving the full definition: 'R is confluent iff for every term t and every pair u, v with t ->> u and t ->> v, there exists a w with u ->> w and v ->> w (a common reduct).' Refutation strategy (distinct normal forms have no common reduct) is stated and justified. The section closes with an explicit 'No common reduct' paragraph walking through the argument.

G8.notes: §3.3 explicitly identifies the domain: 'The eight cases cover every function symbol of Sigma exactly once, so [·] is total on closed terms ... Hence [·] is a total function ClosedTerms(Sigma) -> Z_{>0}.' Case exhaustion is mentioned.

G9.notes: §4 ends with an explicit '> Oracle cross-check' block: 'sim/output-final.txt under the banner Q3. NON-SN: infinite sequence from f(c) prints t_0..t_8 with their sizes and weights, and asserts that step(t_n, (), "rho6") == t_{n+1} for every n = 0..7. The printed sizes are 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, matching size(t_n) = n + 2.'

G10.notes: §5 'Rigor parity disclosure' is a dedicated closing section listing the five shared structural elements (definitions up front, witness/strategy, explicit rule citations, internal verifications, oracle) across the three Qs, plus a one-paragraph acknowledgement of the irreducible structural asymmetry (Q2 proves a universal; Q1/Q3 refute universals via existential witnesses).

new_issues.description: §4 line 312 displays 't_n = f(body_n)   ->_{rho6, eps, sigma}   f(f(body_n)) * sigma-result.' The '* sigma-result' suffix is a notational remnant. Since body_n is closed, substituting it for the rule variable ?x in the RHS template f(f(?x)) literally yields f(f(body_n)); the next line clarifies this ('The substituted RHS is f(f(body_n))'). Not a mathematical error, but the line would read more cleanly without '* sigma-result' or with it replaced by a clean equality 't_n = f(body_n)   ->_{rho6, eps, sigma}   f(f(body_n))'.

§2's 'Both endpoints are normal forms' reasons from c that 'its only position is eps'. For a general reader it might help to note c has no proper subterms (hence no non-root positions to check). The current phrasing is defensible but terse. Not a rigor defect — just a place where one additional clause would aid readability.

overall_notes: The revision closes all ten gaps identified in the prior audit. High-priority G1 (Q3 recurrence bug) is fully fixed via the body_n = f^n(c) framing: t_n -> t_{n+1} is now derived through the clean chain f(body_n) -> f(f(body_n)) = f(body_{n+1}) = t_{n+1}, which is arithmetically correct and matches the simulator's t_0..t_8 output. High-priority G2 (oracle silence) is closed by a top-of-document oracle declaration plus five in-section cross-check blocks. Medium-priority G3–G7 (context closure induction, well-foundedness, strategy totality argument, NF via head-symbol equality, confluence definition) are each fully spelled out. Low-priority G8–G10 (domain of W, Q3 simulator citation, rigor parity paragraph) are addressed. No banned identifiers. No new mathematical issues were introduced; the two minor nits (N1 'sigma-result' remnant, N2 terse c-is-NF phrasing) are stylistic rather than structural. Oracle simulator re-run: all assertions pass. Weighted score 0.96, comfortably above the 0.85 shipping threshold, with no open high-priority gaps. READY TO SHIP.
